coverage_gaps: examples/predictions.ipynb was NOT in any segment and is entirely unaudited. It is a shipped example artifact, is NOT executed by any CI runner (no nbconvert/jupyter in run.sh, run_ci_examples.sh, or ci_example_launcher.py), so any stale API (result.predict, bundle=, load_dataset) inside it would never be caught. The prompt explicitly flagged this notebook; the audit ignored it entirely.

The 4 hand-written API pages docs/source/api/module_api.md, sklearn_integration.md, storage.md, workspace.md appear in NO segment (segments only counted api/*.rst stubs as '357 autodoc'). These are reachable via api/modules.rst and reference/index.md, contain prose + code that can go stale (e.g. storage.md/workspace.md may document SQLite/Parquet/runs-dir behavior that the audit flagged as inconsistent elsewhere), and were never inspected for API correctness or style.

reference/ has 20 md files but ref-pipeline(9)+ref-operators(10)=19; one reference file is uncovered. The reference/configuration/ subdir (index.md, cache_config.md) and reference/api/session.md and top-level reference/configuration.md were not clearly attributed to a segment, leaving a configuration/session reference page unaudited.

Code-block runnability was asserted as a recommendation ('add an example-as-test CI gate') but never measured. The audit did not establish that NO doc .md code block is executed today (confirmed: no doctest/nbval/literalinclude-exec in conf.py or Makefile). Every wrong-API finding in .md prose is therefore un-gated, which strengthens (but was not stated by) the case.

intersphinx is configured (conf.py:115 maps python/numpy/sklearn/pandas) and the build runs with a large nitpick_ignore list and is NOT nitpicky/-W by default. The audit never checked whether intersphinx cross-references resolve, nor that the nitpick_ignore suppressions hide real broken :class: refs. This is a build-correctness dimension the prompt flagged (intersphinx) that was skipped.

i18n FR/EN: there is no FR/EN duplication (no locale/, no *.fr.md, conf.py has no second language) and no alt-text problem (only 5 markdown image refs, all with alt text, 0 empty). The audit never stated it verified these dimensions; they happen to be clean, but the audit's silence means it cannot claim exhaustiveness on accessibility/i18n.

Screenshots/assets: 30+ PNG/PDF assets exist in docs/source/assets/ (shap_beeswarm, candlestick, heatmap, augment_chart, etc.). The audit never verified whether referenced asset filenames exist on disk or whether any doc references a missing image; asset-reference integrity was not audited.
Versioned docs / RTD version selector and the .readthedocs.yaml-to-conf version coupling beyond the single 'release=0.7.1' string were not examined for whether stable/latest builds diverge.
false_or_unproven_claims: CRITICAL FALSE POSITIVE (rank 4, listed as release-blocker and a top_quick_win): 'Two broken imports in developer transfer-learning examples (ImportError)... examples are integration tests so these fail at import time... turns two ImportError-crashing examples green.' This is FALSE. Both imports (D01_transfer_analysis.py:131 `from nirs4all.operators.transfer import TransferPreprocessingSelector` and D03_pca_geometry.py:88 `from nirs4all.operators.analysis import PreprocPCAEvaluator`) are wrapped in try/except ImportError blocks (D01:131-151 catches ImportError and prints 'TransferPreprocessingSelector not available'). The examples do NOT crash — they run, swallow the ImportError, and continue. Real defect remains (wrong documented module path; the feature is never actually demonstrated), but the asserted mechanism (runtime crash / red CI) is wrong and the severity is overstated to 'critical release blocker'.

CRITICAL FALSE POSITIVE (rank 3, listed as release-blocker / top quick win): 'Uninstantiated transform classes passed into pipeline (runtime crash in a test-example)' at U01_shap_basics.py:81-86 and U03_feature_selection.py:87,152. This is FALSE. nirs4all's pipeline parser INTENTIONALLY accepts bare class types: parser.py:231 documents 'Class types: returns as-is (controller will instantiate)'. Many other passing CI examples pass bare classes (U01_preprocessing_basics, U01_multi_model, U04_visualization, U03_basic_classification, R01_pipeline_syntax). U01_shap_basics.py IS in both CI runners (run.sh:102, run_ci_examples.sh:264) and passes, so the bare-class form cannot be crashing. The 'instantiate each class' fix is unnecessary and the 'runtime crash in a test-example' rationale is incorrect.

FALSE per-segment style claim (style_mandate_rollup and health_table): 'Only two segments are fully clean: user_guide/predictions+deployment and the docs build-config/RTD segment.' user_guide/predictions+deployment is NOT clean — grep finds genuine external-pipeline-variable anti-patterns at making_predictions.md:246 (`pipeline = [MinMaxScaler(), KFold(...)]`) and session_api.md:30 and :217 (`pipeline = [MinMaxScaler(), ShuffleSplit(...)]`). At least 3 violating blocks exist in the segment the audit certified as one of only two clean segments. This proves the style sweep was NOT exhaustively grep-verified and that the headline '250+ blocks / 18 of 20 segments / only 2 clean' undercounts and contains at least one demonstrably wrong per-segment classification.

UNPROVEN aggregate: the precise violation counts ('39/41', '~42 blocks', '61 grep-counted', '44 blocks', '250+') are presented as measured but the predictions/deployment miscount shows the methodology was inconsistent across segments. These numbers should be re-derived by a single uniform grep before being used to scope a mechanical sweep.

POTENTIALLY OVERSTATED: rank 12 (y_column vs target_column in faq.md) is asserted as a runtime error, but DatasetConfigs.__init__ was not confirmed to reject the kwarg (it may accept **kwargs and silently ignore, producing wrong behavior rather than a TypeError). The string is genuinely wrong vs target_column, but whether it 'errors' was not verified to source.
missed_or_underweighted: The audit's own credibility is undermined by two false 'critical release blockers' (rank 3 and rank 4) that would NOT block release because both are inside try/except or use a supported bare-class idiom. Acting on these (instantiating bare classes, 'turning red CI green') would be wasted or harmful work and signals the example-as-test claims were not actually executed/verified against the CI runners.

examples/predictions.ipynb (a Jupyter notebook shipped as an example) is invisible to both the audit and CI. This is the single largest coverage hole: an un-run, un-audited user-facing artifact that can silently rot. Underweighted to zero.

The style-mandate true scope is LARGER than reported, not smaller: a segment certified 'clean' actually has violations. The corpus-wide count and the '2 clean segments' framing are too optimistic; a uniform re-grep is required before sizing the sweep.

No doc .md code block is executed anywhere in CI (only examples/*.py run). The audit recommends an example-as-test gate but does not state the current zero-coverage baseline, which is the actual reason every prose API error (result.predict, bundle=, plot_predictions, SklearnWrapper, examples.synthetic) has survived. This baseline should be the headline justification for a doc-snippet CI gate.

The hand-written api/*.md pages (storage.md, workspace.md, module_api.md, sklearn_integration.md) sit at the API reference layer, are reachable from toctrees, and were never audited — yet the audit separately flags storage/workspace inconsistencies (runs/ directory) in OTHER files. These four pages likely carry the same defects and were skipped.

intersphinx + a large nitpick_ignore list + non-nitpicky default build means broken Python cross-references are actively suppressed. The audit's RTD/build segment scored build scripts 'sound' without checking whether nitpick_ignore is masking real autodoc/xref breakage — a hygiene gap the audit underweighted.
verdict: NOT exhaustive enough to base a restructuring on without one more verification pass. The audit is strong on metadata drift (license/version — independently confirmed: result.predict at getting_started/index.md:192, bundle= at deployment/index.md:85, y_column in faq.md, plot_predictions/residuals/calibration absent, examples.synthetic import all TRUE) and on the front-door duplication theme. But it contains at least two FALSE 'critical release blockers' (rank 3 uninstantiated-classes and rank 4 transfer-learning ImportError) that do not crash because of try/except guards and the supported bare-class parser idiom (parser.py:231), and at least one FALSE 'clean segment' certification (predictions+deployment has external-pipeline anti-patterns). It also entirely missed examples/predictions.ipynb (unaudited, un-CI'd), the 4 hand-written api/*.md pages, intersphinx/nitpick-suppression build hygiene, and the fact that zero doc .md code blocks are executed in CI. The single highest-value next pass: (1) actually run the example suite / read the try-except guards before labeling any example a 'runtime crash', and (2) do ONE uniform grep for `pipeline = [` across all of docs/source and examples/ to produce a single authoritative violation count and a correct list of truly-clean files — then audit predictions.ipynb and the api/*.md pages that were never opened.
